domain: Yoruba dùndún talking drum (hourglass double-membrane West African drum; three pitch tones H/M/L match the three relative pitches of Yoruba tonal language; drummer slides leather thongs to tension drum head; intelligible spoken content transmitted via 3-tone surrogate)
specific_mechanism: Continuous pitch quantised to 3 categorical bands matching the language's tonal structure; intelligible spoken content transmits through the 3-band channel with high fidelity.
llm_application: DUNDUN-TONE-BUCKET: a low-resource SSL pretraining target for tonal-language speech models. Instead of dense spectrogram reconstruction or contrastive feature matching, the model is pretrained to predict a SEQUENCE OF 3-SYMBOL PITCH BUCKETS (H/M/L) per frame, computed via cheap F0 extraction + log-mean classification. The 3-bucket target is a categorical low-bandwidth bottleneck (≈log2(3) ≈ 1.6 bits/frame target vs ~512 for spectrogram). Fine-tuning then specialises to ASR/TTS. Differs from Wav2Vec2's masked-spectrogram contrastive objective and CLAP's audio-text contrastive by an EXPLICIT 3-CLASS CATEGORICAL PITCH-TONE OBJECTIVE matching tonal-language structure.
novelty_claim: Wav2Vec2/HuBERT/data2vec use masked-frame prediction with continuous targets. CLAP uses contrastive audio-text. WavLink uses Matryoshka multi-res losses. WavLM/Whisper use mixed contrastive+CTC. DUNDUN-TONE-BUCKET differs by (a) DISCRETE 3-CLASS PITCH-TONE TARGET, (b) deliberately matched to TONAL-LANGUAGE structure (Yoruba/Igbo/Cantonese/Mandarin/Vietnamese), (c) EXTREME LOW-BANDWIDTH bottleneck forces tonal-structure-first learning. The drum surrogacy demonstrates this works for human listeners — applying same bottleneck to SSL pretraining is the novel transfer.
